name: flat-config
context: ESLint flat config format reference — eslint.config.js structure, config objects, cascading, and migration from eslintrc
info: Flat config is ESLint 9+'s default configuration system, replacing the legacy .eslintrc format. A flat config file (eslint.config.js, .mjs, or .cjs) exports an array of configuration objects. Each object can specify files, ignores, rules, plugins, languageOptions, and linterOptions. Objects cascade in order — later objects override earlier ones for matching files. This node explains the format, cascading behavior, key differences from eslintrc, and migration path. Agents use this context when they need to understand why flat config works differently from eslintrc, how config objects cascade, and how to migrate legacy configurations.

sections.title: Config object structure
sections.body: Each config object can have: `files` (glob patterns for targeting), `ignores` (glob patterns to skip), `languageOptions` (parser, ecmaVersion, sourceType, globals), `plugins` (imported plugin objects), `rules` (rule severities and options), `linterOptions` (noInlineConfig, reportUnusedDisableDirectives), `settings` (shared settings for plugins), `processor` (for non-JS files).

sections.title: Cascading behavior
sections.body: Config objects apply in array order — later objects override earlier ones for matching files. A config object without `files` applies to all files. A config object with only `ignores` creates global ignores. This replaces eslintrc's `overrides` array — each config object in flat config IS an override.

sections.title: Migration from eslintrc
sections.body: Use `npx @eslint/migrate-config .eslintrc.json` for automated conversion. Key changes: `extends` becomes spread configs, `env` becomes `globals`, plugins are imported objects not strings, `overrides` become separate config objects in the array. Use `FlatCompat` from `@eslint/eslintrc` to wrap legacy configs that haven't been updated.

sections.title: Key differences from eslintrc
sections.body: Flat config always acts as `root: true` — no upward directory traversal. Plugins are JavaScript objects, not strings. `extends` is replaced by spreading config arrays. `env` is replaced by the `globals` package. `.eslintignore` is replaced by the `ignores` key. `overrides` are replaced by multiple config objects. The `package.json` `eslintConfig` field is no longer supported.
